ops.title: slop-cop check flag is --lang (not --markdown) since v0.1.14; 'unknown flag: --lang' = a stale binary, not a docs bug; two installed binaries + installer fast-path gotcha
ops.body:
The `slop-cop check` language flag is **`--lang`** (`auto|text|markdown|html|jsx|tsx|ts|js`),
shipped in release **v0.1.14**. It replaced the old `--markdown` (`auto|on|off`) flag in commit
`90f9f44`, which landed *after* tag `v0.1.10`. So `slop-cop: unknown flag: --lang` means a
**stale binary**, not a docs/skill bug â€” docs/skills (cc-factory AGENTS.md, slop-cop/writing-docs/
repo-bootstrap skills) correctly use `--lang=markdown`.

Two slop-cop binaries exist on this machine:
- **Live**: `~/.claude/plugins/data/slop-cop-skills/bin/slop-cop` â€” what the skill uses; refreshed
  by `install-binary.sh` (downloads `/releases/latest`). Currently v0.1.14, has `--lang`.
- **Stale fallback**: `~/.claude/plugins/cache/slop-cop/slop-cop/0.1.0/bin/slop-cop` â€” v0.1.10
  (commit `2355d4c`), only knows `--markdown`. Bundled in the cached plugin (manifest 0.1.0);
  the skill's tier-3 fallback (used only if the live binary is missing). **Not on PATH.**

Gotchas:
- `install-binary.sh` **fast-paths out without downloading** if any working binary already exists
  (`$BIN_PATH version` exits 0). To force an upgrade: `rm` the target binary first, then run with
  `CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT=<dir-whose-/bin-holds-the-binary>` (e.g. the `plugins/data/slop-cop-skills`
  dir) so it writes back to the same place.
- The rtk Bash hook (`rtk hook claude`) does **not** rewrite `slop-cop` (only known commands like
  `git`), and slop-cop isn't on PATH â€” so a bare `slop-cop ...` in the Bash-tool env is
  `command not found`, not a slop-cop error.

See [[slop-cop-repo]] for source location, build, and the auto-release model.

**Inversion caveat (2026-07-22 audit, adversarially verified):** in the recent transcript corpus, "unknown flag" errors were dominated by *hallucinated or wrong-subcommand flags against CURRENT binaries* (e.g. `ccx --budget` on a subcommand that takes it elsewhere, `task add --title`), with ZERO stale-binary cases in an 8-sample read-back. Check `<tool> --version` / `--help` before assuming staleness â€” the stale-binary explanation above is the exception now, not the default.
